codeReview.summary: Fail. The landed feature correctly propagates explicit trace metadata through the direct `OpenAIProvider` path and the follow-up commit stabilizes the trace-provider tests, but the new provider hook design is incomplete for wrapper providers. `Runner` now calls `ModelProvider::resolve_trace_metadata()` before trace creation and `ModelProvider::prepare_request()` before model execution, yet `MultiProvider` still only overrides `resolve()`. When callers use `MultiProvider` with an OpenAI-backed route (including the public `openai/...` alias), the OpenAI harness-registration metadata never reaches either exported trace metadata or request metadata, so the feature does not fully satisfy the provider-level parity contract on a public code path.

issues.description: `MultiProvider` was not updated to forward the new `ModelProvider::resolve_trace_metadata()` and `prepare_request()` hooks that this feature added. Because `Runner` invokes those hooks on the top-level provider (`crates/agents-core/src/run.rs:435` and `crates/agents-core/src/run.rs:1262`), a run configured with `MultiProvider` and an OpenAI route such as `openai/gpt-5` skips the OpenAI harness merge entirely: `agent_harness_id` is absent from both the trace metadata and the outbound request metadata even though the selected underlying provider is `OpenAIProvider`. The new tests only cover direct `OpenAIProvider` usage, so this public wrapper-provider path regressed unnoticed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared mission/library guidance does not call out that adding default methods to `ModelProvider` requires auditing wrapper providers such as `MultiProvider` to forward the new behavior. That omission likely contributed to the missed parity path here.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: This feature introduced `resolve_trace_metadata()` and `prepare_request()` in `crates/agents-core/src/models/interface.rs:115-124`, but `crates/agents-core/src/models/multi_provider.rs:136-160` still only implements `resolve()`, so provider-specific metadata behavior is lost behind the wrapper.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, both feature handoffs, the worker transcript skeleton, commit `d35c583`, and the relevant core/OpenAI code paths. The feature does not pass scrutiny because provider-specific trace/request metadata propagation is incomplete for the public `MultiProvider` path, so OpenAI harness metadata parity is not preserved for routed OpenAI models.
